title: hymenophyllaceae
intro: The Hymenophyllaceae rank refers to a family of plants, specifically a group of ferns within the subclass Polypodiidae. This family is characterized by its unique leaf structure, with leaves often being forked or divided into two distinct lobes. The Hymenophyllaceae family includes approximately 150 species, many of which are epiphytic, meaning they grow on other plants such as trees. These plants are typically found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world, often in humid environments with high levels of rainfall.
